what_it_does: This file is the living operational-status register for the company (Vi composition suite) project. It is meant to be read after AGENTS.md and MAP.md so a fresh AI or operator can learn what is currently built, what acceptance tests prove it, what changed recently, and what remains open without re-deriving context.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares type=state, register=descriptive, module=root, aliases, tags, status=living.
Dated as of 2026-06-07, with a long recent-change block covering model/VRAM sizing, voice co-residence, persona identity, and durable voice tracing.
Contains an auto-maintained acceptance-suite list of 232 suites bracketed by SUITES:START/SUITES:END.
Emphasizes that tests/*.py are the convergence record and that claims must be proven by live use, not just asserted.

Maps each built capability to the specific acceptance files that prove it (engine, fabric, registries, RHM, self-modification, decision wire, up-translate, voice, ops, session supervisor, mirror-registry).
Flags unfinished work and open questions inline (e.g., voice trial live verification, FE surfaces, mirror-registry remaining lanes).
Uses GitHub markdown callouts for important rules and warnings.
Provided content is truncated, cutting off the mirror-registry introspection-core section.
standouts: 232 acceptance suites tracked as the live convergence record.
Decision→implementation wire with git checkpoint, surfaced review, and fail-loud guards.
Voice backend with multi-engine TTS routing, VRAM lifecycle convergence, and durable trial recording.
Mirror-Registry System contracts and platform-agnostic introspection-core added 2026-06-13.
RHM uses native tool-calling from a single-source verb spec and carries grounded interface context.
conventions: Auto-generated markers protecting the acceptance-suite list from hand-editing.
Inline test references and status emojis (✅/🟡) to signal proven vs pending.
Repeated principles: fail-loud, one-source, operator-only, prove-by-use, review surfaces.
Maintained by integration rather than append-only.
concerns: Extremely long, dense prose mixing status, rationale, and test mapping; hard to scan.
Truncated in the provided excerpt, so completeness and freshness cannot be verified from this file alone.
The 'current and true' mandate conflicts with the risk of manual prose drift alongside auto-generated lists.
Ad-hoc section dating and emoji flags may become inconsistent if not regenerated centrally.
notes: Read this after the rules and map to know what's built, what's proven, and what's still open.
questions: Which command or script regenerates the auto-maintained acceptance-suite list?
How are the 'built and proven' claims continuously verified—CI, manual run, or a test runner?
What is the full content after the truncation, especially for the remaining mirror-registry lanes?
Which file or process owns authoritative date/version updates to this register?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be a self-contained current-status entry point; the content matches, but its length and truncation make it less immediately scannable than the stated ideal.
apparent_themes: Prove by use and acceptance tests as the convergence record.
Fail-loud, single-source-of-truth, operator-consent, and review surfaces.
Capability-driven development with explicit test mapping.
Governance and safety around autonomous modification.
intention_signals: It tries to make the project's build state transparent and actionable for both humans and AI agents, ensuring no one has to reconstruct context and that every capability claim is tied to a test or live-use evidence.
novelty: Distinctive in treating project status as a first-class, living, machine-readable register with an auto-maintained acceptance-suite inventory and an explicit built-vs-not-built mapping tied to tests.
contribution: Provides a single narrative of the current build state and an enumerated acceptance-suite inventory that a fused system can use to orient new agents and verify capability coverage.
summary_for_embedding: STATE.md is the living status register for the Vi composition suite, summarizing what is built, what is proven by acceptance tests, what changed recently, and what is still pending, with auto-maintained suite lists and governance notes.
intention_for_embedding: It was created so a fresh AI or operator can enter the repository and understand the current build state without re-deriving context or crossing boundaries.
